economic_notes: Frog Lake First Nation has extensive business enterprises including energy resources (Frog Lake Energy Resources Corp and LP, Frog Lake Midstream LP), oilfield services, off-reserve housing, travel centre, gravel and concrete, ranching, construction, agriculture, investments, and TMX pipeline participation. Also holds minority interests in Seven Lakes Oilfield Services, Pimee Well Servicing, and Treaty Six Carbon Hub. GBEs generated net income of $11,154,524 in 2024.
notes: Audit is QUALIFIED on two bases: (1) asset retirement obligations not fully estimated/recorded per Note 14, and (2) insufficient audit evidence on carrying amount and valuation of certain investments. Morning Sky Health and Wellness Society is fully consolidated. 23 business enterprises accounted for by modified equity method including energy, housing, construction, ranching, and TMX pipeline participation. Prior year restated for insufficient ISC funding recognition (+$845,264 to accumulated surplus). Large deferred revenue balance of $39.6M in 2024 vs $10.2M in 2023, primarily ISC funds received but not yet expended. Federal trust funds of $8,674,596 held by Government of Canada (ISC). CMHC replacement reserve of $812,361 is unfunded. Total government transfers calculated as ISC $34,518,198 + ISETS $667,231 + CMHC $170901 = $35,356,330. Restricted cash includes Province of Alberta $629,174 and Legal $102,847.
